stories.title: Molinare hires Des Carey for Dublin studio
stories.summary: Molinare Creative Group has appointed Des Carey as Executive Director, Growth & Innovation, based at their Dublin-based studio, Elephant Goldfish.
stories.why_it_matters: The appointment signals a strategic focus on international expansion and the integration of emerging production technologies, leveraging Carey's extensive background in global cinema innovation and post-production workflows.
verified_facts: Des Carey has been appointed as Executive Director, Growth & Innovation at Elephant Goldfish, part of the Molinare Creative Group.
Carey previously held senior roles at Company 3, Light Iron, Resurgent Film Group, and Samsung.
The role involves leading strategic growth and partnership initiatives across the Molinare Creative Group.
warnings: The source material contains a date reference of 16.07.26, which appears to be a future-dated or placeholder timestamp.

drafts.concise: Molinare Creative Group has announced the appointment of Des Carey as Executive Director, Growth & Innovation. Based at the group’s Dublin studio, Elephant Goldfish, Carey will lead strategic growth and partnership initiatives. With over 25 years of experience across production and post-production, including senior roles at Samsung and Light Iron, Carey will work closely with the wider Molinare team to identify opportunities in film, television, and emerging production technologies. This appointment marks a significant step in the group’s commitment to expanding its international presence and technical capabilities. #Molinare #PostProduction #MediaInnovation
drafts.investor: Molinare Creative Group continues to strengthen its international footprint with the appointment of Des Carey as Executive Director, Growth & Innovation. Based at the group’s Dublin-based studio, Elephant Goldfish, Carey brings extensive experience in cinematic innovation and cloud-based production infrastructure. His remit includes driving strategic growth and identifying new commercial opportunities across the group’s film and television operations. By integrating high-level expertise in emerging technologies, Molinare is positioning itself to address the evolving demands of the global production landscape. This strategic hire underscores the group’s focus on scaling its service offering and enhancing its competitive position in the post-production sector. #Investment #MediaGrowth #Molinare
drafts.people: We are pleased to see Molinare Creative Group welcome Des Carey to the team as Executive Director, Growth & Innovation. Based at Elephant Goldfish in Dublin, Des brings a wealth of international experience, having worked with industry-leading filmmakers and held senior positions at companies including Samsung and Light Iron. His deep expertise in next-generation cinema technology and virtual production will be a significant asset as the group continues to evolve its creative and technical offerings. We look forward to seeing the impact of his leadership on the group’s strategic development and collaborative initiatives across the business. #Leadership #PostProduction #Molinare

sector_stories.title: Royal Mail admits massive delivery chaos is hitting dozens of UK towns today – is YOUR postcode on the dreaded list? - The Sun
sector_stories.summary: Royal Mail has acknowledged significant delivery disruptions affecting service levels across numerous UK towns.
sector_stories.angle: Operational failures at the incumbent market leader create potential volume displacement and service-level benchmarking opportunities for competitors.

sector_stories.title: Royal Mail delivery delays hit 10 Cambridge postcodes today full list
sector_stories.summary: Royal Mail is experiencing service delays across 141 UK locations, including 10 Cambridge postcodes, due to local issues such as high sick absence and resourcing challenges.
sector_stories.angle: Competitor service instability creates potential opportunities for alternative delivery providers to capture displaced volume and improve market share.

sector_stories.title: Government announce increased tax credit for films with big visual effects budgets
sector_stories.summary: The Irish government has introduced a new Visual Effects Uplift to the Section 481 Film Tax Credit, offering a 40% credit for qualifying VFX expenditure up to €10 million.
sector_stories.angle: Increased regional tax incentives for VFX may shift international production locations and competitive dynamics within the European post-production market.

sector_stories.title: New uplift in tax credit for film industry , Government confirm
sector_stories.summary: The Irish government has introduced a 40% Film Tax Credit for productions with at least €1 million in visual effects expenditure to boost the domestic VFX sector.
sector_stories.angle: Increased regional competition for VFX and post-production work due to enhanced tax incentives in the Irish market.

sector_stories.title: 'HOPE' Director Na Hong-jin: "There Were Over 400 Creature Shots; I Even Wondered Why I Was Covering the Actors' Faces with CGI" - SBS Star
sector_stories.summary: Director Na Hong-jin reported that the film 'HOPE' required over 400 creature shots, involving extensive use of CGI to cover actors' faces.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights the increasing reliance on complex VFX and creature design in modern high-budget film production workflows.

sector_stories.title: Carbios reaches milestone of 100 batches at its Industrial demonstration plant and expands its licensing offering to the textile market
sector_stories.summary: Carbios has successfully processed 100 batches at its industrial demonstration plant and expanded its enzymatic biorecycling licensing to include PET polyester textile waste.
sector_stories.angle: Advances in enzymatic recycling technology for PET increase the potential availability and quality of recycled rPET feedstock.

sector_stories.title: EU Bans Fashion Waste ; SMX Makes it New
sector_stories.summary: The EU has implemented a ban on the destruction of unsold consumer goods, necessitating verifiable evidence of material origin and circularity for products including textiles and plastics.
sector_stories.angle: Increasing regulatory requirements for material traceability and circularity in the EU will impact PET packaging manufacturers.

sector_stories.title: Samsung in talks to back France Mistral at €20bn valuation
sector_stories.summary: Mistral is in advanced talks to raise several billion euros at a €20bn valuation, with potential backing from Samsung and EQT to scale European sovereign AI infrastructure.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights significant capital inflows and strategic momentum for European sovereign AI alternatives to US-based cloud-dependent models.

sector_stories.title: Microsoft and Mistral Expand AI Partnership with Multibillion - Dollar Europe Compute Investment
sector_stories.summary: Microsoft and Mistral have expanded their partnership to increase European AI compute capacity and provide flexible deployment options, including air-gapped environments for regulated industries.
sector_stories.angle: Validates market demand for sovereign AI infrastructure and highlights competitive pressure from major cloud providers in Europe.

sector_stories.title: Supermicro AI Orders Remain Strong as AI GPU Platforms Generate Over 80 Percent of Revenue
sector_stories.summary: Supermicro reported that AI GPU-based platforms generated over 80 percent of its third-quarter fiscal 2026 revenue, driven by demand from hyperscalers, cloud providers, and sovereign AI projects.
sector_stories.angle: Validates sustained global demand for sovereign AI infrastructure and highlights the growing importance of integrated software management layers.

sector_stories.title: Pro - Life Group Wants More Ethical Limits and Regulations on IVF
sector_stories.summary: Liberty Counsel Action has formally urged the US government to prioritize Restorative Reproductive Medicine over IVF coverage, citing ethical concerns and a need for stricter industry regulation.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights growing political and regulatory pressure on IVF practices, potentially influencing future insurance coverage and clinical oversight standards.
